semanticCore: Small band, remnant of people, minor group, patch, or shredded pieces

keyDerivations.meaning: Small band of people, remnant group, minor detachment, or shredded fragments

figurativeOrRareUsages: إِنَّ هَٰؤُلَاءِ لَشِرْذِمَةٌ قَلِيلُونَ (26:54): [Pharaoh said]: 'Indeed, these (Israelites) are a small band/group.'
summaryEn: The root refers to a small, scattered group or remnant of people (Shirzimah), used by Pharaoh to dismiss and belittle the fleeing Israelites as an insignificant, minor band.
summaryAr: يشير هذا الجذر إلى الشرذمة وهي الطائفة القليلة المتفرقة والقطعة المتبقية من الناس والخرقة الممزقة (شرذم)، واصفاً قول فرعون تحقيراً لبني إسرائيل.
rawText: شرذم شِرْذِمَةٌ A party, or company, (طَائِفَةٌ,) of men, or people: (S:) or a small company: (TA:) or a small number of men, or people: (K:) and so شِرْدِمَةٌ, with the unpointed د, on the authority of AA: (IB, TA:) the former occurring in the Kur xxvi. 54. (TA.) === A piece, or portion, (S, K,) of a thing, (S,) of a quince &c.: pl. شَرَاذِمُ and شَرَاذِيمُ. (K.) === [Hence,] ثَوْبٌ شَرَاذِمُ, (S,) or ثِيَابٌ شَرَاذِمُ, (K,) A garment, or garments, old and worn out, (S, K,) much rent. (K.)
